matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: An observed SIRT6 variant places this sample in the curated SIRT6 research context. The bundled SIRT6 knowledge base is centered on aging and genome-maintenance biology. Most currently relevant human variants are interpreted as research-grade longevity or regulatory signals rather than as routine pathogenic clinical alleles. SIRT6 is a core longevity and genome-stability gene, so common and rare human variants are mainly interpreted through aging and cellular-maintenance phenotypes.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: SIRT6 is heavily studied in aging, genome stability, inflammation, lipid and glucose metabolism, and cardiovascular disease.
Rare centenarian-enriched alleles and selected common SNPs are being used to connect human genetics with SIRT6 functional biochemistry.
Aging and longevity research.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a SIRT6 variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. SIRT6 methylation should be interpreted as chromatin and transcriptional context around an NAD-dependent longevity enzyme. Because SIRT6 function is closely tied to chromatin regulation, methylation can be informative, but not as a one-to-one biomarker. The bundled SIRT6 knowledge base is centered on aging and genome-maintenance biology. Most currently relevant human variants are interpreted as research-grade longevity or regulatory signals rather than as routine pathogenic clinical alleles. SIRT6 is a core longevity and genome-stability gene, so common and rare human variants are mainly interpreted through aging and cellular-maintenance phenotypes.
